programme.title: Winchester Life
programme.desc:
It’s been 60 years since AA was introduced to the shooting sports world. Since that day, an immeasurable number of AA hulls have hit the ground and countless shooters have stood center stage raising a trophy where millions of dollars have been won. Professional and recreational shooters alike have trusted AA for six decades. In this episode, load up for stories of high school, collegiate competitors and the world’s best as they bust clays with the legendary shot shell.
GUESTS: Zach Keenbaum (Team Winchester) Desi Edmunds (Team Winchester) Kayla Wilgus (Team Winchester) Amy Cawley (Team Winchester) Anthony Matarese (Team Winchester)

programme.title: Surviving Mann
programme.desc: With the mission complete, the contestants think the season is winding down—but HQ has other plans. Their once-fabricated cover story becomes reality, pulling them into a pulse-pounding new challenge designed to test strength, coordination, and mastery of weaponry. This hybrid competition pushes every skill to the limit, weeding out all but the most well-rounded tactical athletes. Along the way, the legendary Don Mann shares his own backstory, giving rare insight into the life and mindset of the man behind the mission. When the dust settles, only one competitor will rise above the rest—earning the title of the season’s most complete warrior.

programme.title: Surviving Mann
programme.desc: The competition reaches its ultimate test as the Face-Off enters Phase Two. Contestants are pushed to their breaking points with a brutal mix of tactical and physical challenges—running and gunning through the forest, navigating five tactical evolutions, and grinding through five grueling physical trials, capped by a punishing two-mile run over unforgiving terrain. Along the way, Don reveals the origins of his legendary SEAL Adventure Challenge, the inspiration behind this ultimate proving ground. When one contestant’s body shuts down in shock, the resilience of the warrior mindset is put on full display in a dramatic fight to recover and press on. In this season finale, only the strongest will endure, but every competitor leaves forged by fire.

programme.title: Country Ball
programme.desc: Richard Myers is a versatile offensive lineman with an exceptional work ethic, already college football material. The challenge? He’s from a small Indiana town and struggles to get noticed by recruiters. Join Coach Jesse and Richard as they bond over fishing and an old-fashioned Indiana demolition derby, discussing strategies to build Richard’s football future with Country Ball.

programme.title: Surviving Mann
programme.desc: Back by unpopular demand, the infamous “Blah Line” returns—and it’s more brutal than ever. The contestants kick off the day with a hellish PT session that leaves bodies broken and spirits tested. Things only get rougher from there with a punishing stealth crawl over jagged rocks, grinding them down before they face Ken Shamrock’s infamous Lion’s Den training—a relentless gauntlet of pain, grit, and raw endurance. As Don Mann continues to forge a battle-ready team, the squad is thrown into breaching drills designed for high-stakes mission execution. It’s chaos, it’s calculated, and it separates the contenders from the pretenders. In the heat of the action, two standouts emerge from the ranks—earning the coveted role of designated breachers.

programme.title: All Stars
programme.desc: The challenges have only gotten started. In episode two, the teams must go beyond their limits to come out on top. From the brutal Blah line to building their own shelters with only a small ax, it’s a battle for scoring points and survival that’ll test these elite competitors like never before.

programme.title: S1E1 Draft Story
programme.desc: In Part 1, we uncover the story of Tyvis Powell and the people who shaped him into the man and football player he is today. His road to the NFL was anything but easy, and it nearly never happened without the guidance of his mentor, Sean Williams. A Draft Story is a GoPro Original Production that follows Ohio State safety Tyvis Powell as he makes the leap to professional football. Though he was not invited to Chicago for the 2016 NFL Draft, his journey is unforgettable. Witness the tension, hope, and emotion as Tyvis waits for the call that will change his life and etch the Powell name into NFL history. This is his Draft Story.

programme.title: S1 E2 Draft Story

programme.title: One Eyed Jacks 1961
programme.desc: Marlon Brando directs and stars in One-Eyed Jacks, a gripping Western of betrayal and revenge. As outlaw Rio (Brando) breaks free from prison, he sets his sights on settling the score with his former partner, Dad Longworth (Karl Malden), who double-crossed him years before and now wears the badge of a sheriff. But Rio’s quest for vengeance grows complicated when he falls for Longworth’s spirited daughter, leaving him torn between love and justice. Sweeping vistas, raw emotion, and Brando’s electrifying presence make this a Western unlike any other.

programme.title: Billy the Kid Trapped (1942) Colorized
programme.desc: In Billy the Kid Trapped, Buster Crabbe returns as the notorious outlaw, hunted across the frontier with his pals Fuzzy (Al St. John) and Jeff (Malcolm ‘Bud’ McTaggart). When the trio is framed for crimes they didn’t commit, they must outwit the law, battle ruthless schemers, and fight to clear their names. Packed with fast-paced action, blazing gunfights, and lively camaraderie, this classic Western proves that even a wanted man can ride for justice.

programme.title: Get A Grip
programme.desc: A failed metal designed for gun barrels, becomes kitchen favourite: surgical steel. Messing around in a spectacle maker's shop leads to the telescope. A bag of dirt from a remote island contains a bacteria that helps stop aging. Pulling burrs off his dog's coat leads to the invention of Velcro.

programme.desc: A failed metal designed for gun barrels, becomes kitchen favourite: surgical steel. Messing around in a spectacle maker's shop leads to the telescope.
